text: Team Meeting Notes:

Sprint Planning — Week 24: The team aligned on the goal of shipping the semantic search feature by end of sprint. Backend will implement the embedding pipeline using the OpenAI embeddings API. Frontend will build the search UI with debounced input and result highlighting.
Decisions Made: We decided to use FAISS for local vector storage to avoid external dependencies.
Knowledge base will be refreshed nightly via a cron job.: Chunking strategy will be paragraph-level with a minimum of 40 characters per chunk.

Action Items: Alice: Set up the embedding pipeline and store results to JSON by Wednesday. Bob: Integrate retrieval endpoint into the frontend search component. Carol: Write unit tests for cosine similarity and top-k retrieval logic.
Blockers: The Groq API rate limit may be a concern during bulk embedding. We will batch requests in groups of 20. PDF parsing quality depends on whether the document is text-based or scanned.

AI agents are autonomous systems that can plan, reason, and act toward goals without human intervention. Large Language Models like GPT and Claude are trained on massive text datasets using transformer architecture. Vector databases store high-dimensional embeddings and support fast similarity search using cosine or dot-product distance. RAG stands for Retrieval-Augmented Generation. It combines a retrieval step with a generative model to produce grounded, accurate responses. Prompt engineering is the practice of crafting inputs to guide language model behavior toward desired outputs. Fine-tuning involves training a pretrained model on a smaller domain-specific dataset to improve performance on specific tasks. Hallucination in LLMs refers to the model generating factually incorrect or fabricated information with high confidence. The transformer architecture uses self-attention mechanisms to weigh the importance of different tokens in a sequence.
Productivity & Work Habits:
Time Blocking: Time blocking is a scheduling technique where you divide your day into dedicated blocks for specific tasks. It minimizes context switching and improves deep work output.
The Two-Minute Rule: If a task takes less than two minutes, do it immediately rather than adding it to your to-do list. This
keeps small tasks from piling up.:
Deep Work: Deep work refers to focused, uninterrupted cognitive effort on complex tasks. It produces more value
per hour than shallow, distracted work.:
Weekly Reviews: A weekly review involves reflecting on your progress, clearing your inbox, and planning the upcoming
week. It keeps your goals aligned with your daily actions.:
Energy Management: Managing energy levels is as important as managing time. Schedule your most demanding tasks during your peak energy hours, typically morning for most people.
Single-Tasking: Multitasking reduces the quality of all tasks being performed simultaneously. Focusing on one task at a
time leads to faster completion and fewer errors.:
# AI Research Notes:

## Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF): RLHF is a technique used to align language models with human preferences. It involves training a reward model on human comparisons, then using RL to optimize the LLM against that reward signal.

## Chain of Thought Prompting: Chain of Thought (CoT) prompting encourages models to reason step by step before giving a final answer. It significantly improves performance on multi-step math and logic tasks.

## Agents and Tool Use: Modern AI agents can call external tools like search engines, calculators, and code interpreters. This extends their capabilities far beyond what is possible with language alone.
## Embeddings: Word and sentence embeddings map text to dense vectors in a high-dimensional space. Semantically similar text tends to cluster together, enabling semantic search and retrieval.
## Context Window: The context window defines how many tokens a model can process in a single pass. Larger context windows allow models to reason over longer documents and conversations.
